message:
chore(beads): file bd-d8v93 tracking pre-existing select_model_and_thinking test failures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-cz006","title":"FUZZ-V3: E2E Fuzz Orchestration Script — Master runner with JSONL logging, timing, coverage collection","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkReef","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:16:53.439696977Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T03:48:05.724501519Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T03:48:05.724476502Z","close_reason":"Implemented FUZZ-V3 orchestrator script with unified report/events + documented usage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","fuzz","logging","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cz006","depends_on_id":"bd-1uny7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:14Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-cz006","depends_on_id":"bd-26ecm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:14Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3751,"issue_id":"bd-cz006","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## FUZZ-V3: E2E Fuzz Orchestration Script

### Purpose
Master script that orchestrates the FULL fuzzing pipeline end-to-end:
1. Runs P1 proptest validation (V1)
2. Runs P2 fuzz harness validation (V2)
3. Generates a unified report combining both
4. Produces JSONL event log for every step
5. Serves as the single entry point for developers and CI

### Script Design

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/fuzz_e2e.sh — Master fuzz orchestration
#
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/fuzz_e2e.sh              # Full run (proptests + fuzz harnesses)
#   ./scripts/fuzz_e2e.sh --quick      # Quick run (proptests only, 10s fuzz)
#   ./scripts/fuzz_e2e.sh --deep       # Deep run (proptests + 30min fuzz per target)
#   ./scripts/fuzz_e2e.sh --report     # Just regenerate report from existing logs
```

### JSONL Event Log
Every action emits a structured event to fuzz/reports/events.jsonl:
```jsonl
{"ts":"2026-02-15T10:00:00Z","event":"pipeline_start","mode":"full"}
{"ts":"2026-02-15T10:00:01Z","event":"phase_start","phase":"P1","target_count":30}
{"ts":"2026-02-15T10:00:02Z","event":"proptest_run","module":"sse::tests","fn":"sse_chunking_invariant","cases":256,"status":"pass","time_ms":340}
...
{"ts":"2026-02-15T10:05:00Z","event":"phase_end","phase":"P1","passed":30,"failed":0,"total_cases":5120}
{"ts":"2026-02-15T10:05:01Z","event":"phase_start","phase":"P2","target_count":14}
{"ts":"2026-02-15T10:05:02Z","event":"fuzz_build","status":"pass","time_ms":45000}
{"ts":"2026-02-15T10:05:47Z","event":"fuzz_run","target":"fuzz_sse_parser","time_limit_s":60,"status":"pass","corpus_growth":234}
...
{"ts":"2026-02-15T10:20:00Z","event":"pipeline_end","status":"pass","total_time_ms":1200000}
```

### Unified Report
Combines V1 (proptest) and V2 (fuzz) results into one document:
```json
{
  "pipeline_version": "1.0",
  "timestamp": "2026-02-15T...",
  "mode": "full",
  "phases": {
    "P1_proptest": { ... V1 report ... },
    "P2_libfuzzer": { ... V2 report ... }
  },
  "summary": {
    "overall_status": "pass",
    "proptest_functions": 30,
    "proptest_cases": 5120,
    "fuzz_targets": 14,
    "fuzz_time_s": 840,
    "total_crashes": 0,
    "corpus_total": 3456,
    "bugs_found": 0,
    "bugs_fixed": 3
  }
}
```

### Exit Codes
- 0: All phases pass
- 1: P1 proptest failures
- 2: P2 fuzz crashes found
- 3: Both P1 and P2 failures
- 4: Build failure (infrastructure broken)

### Integration with CI (P3)
The E2E script IS what the CI workflow calls. P3.1 (GitHub Actions) wraps this script with:
- Matrix parallelism for individual fuzz targets
- Caching for corpus persistence
- Artifact upload for crash inputs

### Files to Create
- scripts/fuzz_e2e.sh (main orchestration script)
- fuzz/reports/ directory structure
- Helper: scripts/parse_proptest_output.py (or inline awk/jq)

### Acceptance Criteria
- Script runs full pipeline in under 30 minutes (60s per fuzz target)
- JSONL event log captures every step with timestamps
- Unified JSON report produced at known path
- Script is idempotent (re-running produces fresh results)
- Exit codes correctly reflect pass/fail status
- --quick mode completes in under 5 minutes
- Human-readable terminal output with colored pass/fail indicators","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:19:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-d7ccm","title":"Align remaining RPC test output channels with SyncSender","description":"tests/rpc_protocol.rs and tests/rpc_edge_cases.rs still construct std::sync::mpsc::Sender<String> for rpc::run() even though src/rpc.rs now requires SyncSender<String>, which can break cargo check --all-targets after the RPC output channel change.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:33:45.668532716Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:52.844954625Z","closed_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:52.844931963Z","close_reason":"Aligned remaining RPC test output channels with SyncSender and validated targeted tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-d7gn","title":"Feature: Research & selection of popular Pi extensions","description":"Research and select the global 200+ most-used Pi extensions (incl. OpenClaw ecosystem) using an explicit popularity rubric, reproducible discovery queries, and evidence-backed tiering.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:00:25.249934256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:37:31.192922977Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:37:30.776726156Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","research","selection"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-d7gn","depends_on_id":"bd-po15","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:02Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2768,"issue_id":"bd-d7gn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Purpose
Define and execute the research + selection methodology for the expanded extension corpus. This is the foundation for all downstream pinning, gap analysis, and conformance work.

Key Questions
- What does “popular” mean in this ecosystem? (stars, forks, usage, mentions, downloads, recency)
- Which sources are authoritative? (pi-mono, GitHub orgs, npm packages, community lists)
- How do we ensure coverage across extension types, complexity, and capability surface?

Outputs
- A documented scoring rubric with explicit thresholds and coverage quotas.
- A candidate inventory list with metadata per extension (repo, commit/tag, license, type, deps, runtime tier, capabilities).
- Tier-1/Tier-2 selection with justification for each chosen extension.

Constraints
- The final corpus must be runnable unmodified; if something breaks, we fix the runtime/shims, not the extension.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:12:10Z"},{"id":2769,"issue_id":"bd-d7gn","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"RESEARCH METHOD (2026-02-05)

Goal
- Discover, validate, and select the global set of “most used” Pi extensions (target: 200+ must-pass) across GitHub, npm, official Pi sources, and OpenClaw/marketplace ecosystems.

Critical definition: “True Pi extension”
A candidate is only considered a Pi extension if it is compatible with Pi’s extension protocol (or can be made compatible via *generic* pipeline transforms):
- Exposes an entrypoint consistent with Pi’s extension API (e.g., default export taking an ExtensionAPI / ctx-like object).
- Uses recognizable Pi extension registration patterns (tools, slash commands, event hooks, provider registration, UI/RPC calls).
- NOT merely “agent prompts”, “Claude Code plugins”, or unrelated MCP servers unless they explicitly implement Pi’s extension protocol.

Evidence-backed popularity (rubric anchor)
- Use bd-29ko rubric as the baseline.
- Extend evidence sources for new ecosystems:
  - Marketplace rank/installs (OpenClaw/ClawHub or analogous)
  - GitHub stars/forks + recent activity
  - npm downloads (weekly) + dependents
  - Cross-repo mentions (README/issue references)

Selection output (what downstream work expects)
- A machine-readable candidate inventory (raw, unfiltered) + a validated/deduped inventory.
- A tiered selection with explicit quotas:
  - Tier-1 >=200 (MUST PASS)
  - Tier-2 additional stretch / long-tail coverage
- Every chosen extension includes:
  - source pin (repo URL + commit/tag + path)
  - license status
  - declared interaction model + capabilities
  - scenario linkage (what conformance tests must do)

Anti-goals / pitfalls
- No one-off, per-extension compatibility hacks.
- No silent exclusions: if something is popular but incompatible, it becomes an explicit gap with an explicit reason.
- Avoid confusing other ecosystems’ “extensions” with Pi extensions; validate via code signatures before counting.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:03:16Z"},{"id":2770,"issue_id":"bd-d7gn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closed. Research & selection completed via bd-2hap (research) + bd-3o8d (selection) + bd-2oal (acquisition). 223 extensions across 5 source tiers validated and catalogued in docs/extension-catalog.json.","created_at":"2026-02-07T06:37:31Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-d8v93","title":"Regression: select_model_and_thinking tests return cloudflare-ai-gateway instead of openai-codex","description":"Seven tests in src/app.rs::tests fail on main as of 2902a2e3 (and previous commits eedec079, 6172854a, etc. — pre-existing for at least the last 7 commits):

- app::tests::select_model_and_thinking_preserves_restore_warning_when_defaulting_for_setup
- app::tests::select_model_and_thinking_preserves_restore_warning_when_using_config_default
- app::tests::select_model_and_thinking_restores_model_from_header_when_history_missing
- app::tests::select_model_and_thinking_restores_thinking_from_active_branch_only
- app::tests::select_model_and_thinking_restores_thinking_from_header_when_history_missing
- rpc::tests::auto_compaction_missing_api_key_omits_absent_result_field
- session::tests::test_continue_recent_in_dir_refreshes_index_after_changed_disk_session

The select_model_and_thinking_* failures all manifest as:
  left: 'cloudflare-ai-gateway'
  right: 'openai-codex'

Tests register a setup model (test_model_entry('gpt-5.4', 'openai-codex')) into the registry, expect default_model_from_candidates to find it via the ('openai-codex', 'gpt-5.4') entry in the priority list at src/app.rs:765-797. Instead the code returns a cloudflare-ai-gateway model, suggesting:

1. The merged setup entry is being filtered out of registry.models() somehow, OR
2. canonical_provider_id is no longer canonicalizing 'openai-codex' to itself, OR
3. The defaults priority list iteration is short-circuiting at a different match

a444a157 (2026-04-17 'tests: decouple test fixtures from production model ids…') updated ONE test fixture to use 'test-gpt-5.4' but did not migrate the other tests that still reference the production 'gpt-5.4' / 'openai-codex' pair.

Verified pre-existing by running the same test on commit eedec079 (4 commits before this triage session): same failure. Therefore unrelated to v0.1.14 changes (claude-opus-4-7 snapshot, model override config, clippy).

Did NOT block v0.1.14 release because pre-existing and not user-facing.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:19.078046993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:19.078046993Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

- Guidance on how to add future extensions.

# Notes
Docs should mirror catalog and conformance results.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:38:59.789558100Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:19:21.652115613Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:19:21.092036107Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dbgq","depends_on_id":"bd-1chv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-dbgq","depends_on_id":"bd-2ntq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-dbgq","depends_on_id":"bd-3a24","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:05Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2995,"issue_id":"bd-dbgq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: Users need plain‑language docs alongside the raw catalog.

Reasoning: Documentation explains how to use extensions and what is guaranteed.

Considerations: Keep docs synchronized with the catalog and conformance results.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:53:46Z"},{"id":2996,"issue_id":"bd-dbgq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closed. Updated EXTENSIONS.md §1C.5 (achieved coverage tables), §6 (conformance harness with differential oracle), §7 (performance budgets/baselines), §8.1 (known limitations), §8.2 (supported Node shims), §9 (extension onboarding guide), §10 (future work). Updated README.md: extensions section from Planned to working, architecture diagram includes Extension Manager, limitations table and FAQ updated.","created_at":"2026-02-07T06:19:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-dhka","title":"Unit tests: buffer_shim.rs (node:buffer compatibility)","description":"Add #[cfg(test)] unit tests for src/buffer_shim.rs covering Buffer.from, Buffer.alloc, Buffer.concat, encoding conversions (utf-8/hex/base64), slice/copy semantics, and integration with QuickJS runtime.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FuchsiaWolf","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:44:11.619505234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:44:56.041623817Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T21:44:47.564022218Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","shims","testing"],"comments":[{"id":3780,"issue_id":"bd-dhka","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Skipped: buffer_shim.rs is a pure JS constant (NODE_BUFFER_JS). No Rust logic to unit test. JS correctness should be tested via QuickJS integration tests or conformance suite.","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:44:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-dj27","title":"Release: versioning + tag strategy (pre-1.0 → 1.0)","description":"# Goal
Define the single source of truth for versioning and tags so releases are repeatable and humans don’t have to remember tribal knowledge.

# Background
- This repo ships a `pi` binary and also exposes a library crate (`pi`).
- Publishing to crates.io and publishing GitHub Releases both need a consistent version scheme.

# Decisions to Make (write down explicitly)
1) **When do we call it 1.0?**
   - What is the minimum feature set / stability bar?
2) **Version coupling:**
   - Are `pi_agent_rust` crate version, `pi` binary `--version`, and any published sibling crates (asupersync/rich_rust/charmed_rust) versioned together or independently?
3) **Tag format:**
   - e.g. `vX.Y.Z` tags for repo releases.
4) **Changelog source:**
   - Prefer generating `CHANGELOG.md` from closed beads via `br changelog` (see sibling task).

# Output / Deliverables
- A short doc section (can live in `docs/releasing.md`) describing:
  - version bump procedure
  - tag naming
  - how to cut a patch release
  - what is required before tagging (quality gates + CI green)

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A human can follow the written steps and create a release without guessing.
- [ ] Version/tag policy is consistent with existing `.github/workflows/publish.yml` and crate metadata.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:20:59.131169345Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:27:19.729663150Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T00:42:50.759173199Z","close_reason":"Added docs/releasing.md with version/tag policy + release steps (commit 99b00e4)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dj27","depends_on_id":"bd-gqtd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
